items.title: CEO Interview with Mike Horton of HYFIX Spatial Intelligence
items.summary: Mike Horton is a co-founder of HYFIX Spatial Intelligence, which builds GNSS hardware for decentralized positioning and timing networks,  and GEODNET, the world’s largest decentralized GNSS reference network, and HYFIX Spatial Intelligence, which builds GNSS hardware for decentralized positioning and tim

items.title: （獨家）晶片產能滿手、銅牆終將倒下？　Marvell營運長談AI光學互連的下一步
items.summary: 美系晶片大廠邁威爾（Marvell）在COMPUTEX 2026期間，一改過往低調風格，執行長Matt Murphy發表主題演講、高層也都來到台灣，完整說明AI資料中心的連接技術與市場前景。本次DIGITIMES獨家專訪Marvell

items.title: （獨家）NVIDIA AI伺服器架構散熱趨彈性　兩片式均熱片朝「可拆卸」方向設計
items.summary: NVIDIA Vera Rubin（VR）AI伺服器架構的散熱設計「臨陣換將」備受矚目。供應鏈業者透露，原本兩片式設計改回單片設計，預計本月（2026年6月）定案，改回單片幾成定局。伺服器業界觀察，由於晶片散熱需求持續

items.title: 零組件漲價壓力浮現　三星2026折疊機出貨目標趨保守
items.summary: 三星電子（Samsung Electronics）已制定2026年3款折疊式手機新機出貨計畫，合計500萬~600萬支。相較於2025年折疊新機約600萬支的出貨量，此目標顯得較為保守。根據韓媒ZDNet Korea引述多位零組件業界人

items.title: AI代理世代消費終端兩樣情：PC枯木逢春、手機廠陷成本困局
items.summary: NVIDIA執行長黃仁勳宣布從2026年秋季開始，將攜手PC品牌陸續推出搭載RTX Spark超級晶片的NB及PC，與微軟（Microsoft）、Arm三方聯手，進軍AI PC戰場。NVIDIA盼AI PC不再只是一個調用雲端模型的終

items.title: （獨家）地獄與天堂只差2吋？SiC基板共援800V HVDC需求　6吋虧本築牆、8吋開闢獲利賽道
items.summary: 驅動電動車（BEV）與AI資料中心（AIDC）800V高壓直流（HVDC）浪潮的背後，第三類半導體碳化矽（SiC）基板材料正兵分兩路發展，呈現截然不同的市場樣貌。6吋產品以低價甚至虧本銷售支撐市場需求；8吋產品

items.title: 博通2027衝AI晶片1,000億美元未變　Google TPU轉單成隱憂？
items.summary: 博通（Broadcom）6月3日發布截至5月3日的2026會計年度第2季（2QFY26）財報，營收年增48%、達222億美元，但未能達到市場上對該公司客製化AI晶片業務需求的預期，導致4日市值暴跌超過12.6%，市值蒸發約2,860

items.title: NVIDIA與微軟聯手改寫AI版圖　南韓憂遭邊緣化、淪為零件供應商
items.summary: NVIDIA攜手微軟（Microsoft）推出重新定義個人電腦（PC）架構的AIPC超級晶片RTX Spark，正大大震撼圍繞AI霸權展開的全球科技大廠版圖。韓媒Edaily等報導，硬體絕對霸主NVIDIA與掌控全球電腦作業系統

items.title: 雲端AI帳單愈燒愈高　微軟要讓Windows終端分攤算力成本
items.summary: 微軟（Microsoft）執行長Satya Nadella日前在最新深度訪談中坦言，AI時代下微軟正透過自研大模型（LLM）奪回底層控制權，並明確拒絕為了短期雲端收入出賣算力「賺快錢」，他還預言傳統SaaS將全面轉向「訂閱+

items.title: 法國攬逾1100億歐元AI投資　審批程序考驗落地成效
items.summary: 法國總統馬克宏（Emmanuel Macron）在「選擇法國」（Choose France）投資峰會上宣布攬獲逾1,100億歐元（約1,280億美元）的人工智慧（AI）與資料中心投資承諾，但電網瓶頸與行政障礙恐讓龐大承諾難兌現。綜

items.title: 數位分身卡位AI工廠　達梭、雲達攜NVIDIA搶攻工業級部署
items.summary: 為加速AI驅動企業營運與研發製造創新，COMPUTEX2026展場中除AI晶片、自動化機器人、系統解決方案等，今年不乏業者展示數位分身（Digital Twins）的應用與發展，做為加速工業數位轉型以及提升生產彈性與效

items.title: Marvell押注矽光子　AI資料中心互連迎十年重構
items.summary: 隨著人工智慧（AI）模型規模持續擴張，資料中心面臨的最大瓶頸已不再只是GPU效能，而是晶片之間的資料傳輸能力。為此，Marvell執行長Matt Murphy於COMPUTEX 2026主題演講中指出，傳統銅線互連正逐步逼近

items.title: M5 Pro拆解揭Mac晶片戰略轉向　蘋果強化GPU與記憶體備戰AI PC
items.summary: 就在2026年蘋果（Apple）WWDC大會倒數幾天前，據Counterpoint Research的拆解分析顯示，蘋果M5 Pro晶片內部架構預示著蘋果晶片戰略的重大轉變。顯示蘋果正在調整其Mac晶片戰略，以適應AI PC市場，舉凡

items.title: 中韓新世代面板戰開打：京東方月底量產8.6代OLED　首批供貨華碩、宏碁
items.summary: 中國面板業者京東方將於6月17日舉行8.6代OLED生產線量產儀式，韓廠三星顯示器（Samsung Display；SDC）計劃7月開始量產8.6代OLED，京東方、SDC將從技術追趕進入量產正面交鋒，8.6代OLED世代競爭正式

items.title: x86處理器1Q26出貨下滑超越往年　超微伺服器CPU逆勢突圍
items.summary: 根據最新研調數據，2026年第1季全球x86處理器出貨量下滑逾6%，但伺服器CPU逆勢突圍，其中超微（AMD）的表現最為亮眼。而Arm架構處理器的出貨佔比則微幅成長。根據The Register報導，Mercury Research研究

items.title: CSP巨擘搶完2027記憶體LTA產能　AI動能再鎖定至2028
items.summary: 隨著NVIDIA新一代AI加速器Vera Rubin將於2026年下半出貨，HBM4投入量產即將火力全開，CSP大廠亦持續重金斥資投入AI資料中心建置，多家記憶體供應鏈指出，2027年缺貨恐將比2026年更為嚴峻，CSP大廠不僅

items.title: 晶片通膨看全球AI記憶體熱　上游握定價權、下游承壓配給制
items.summary: AI不只吸走GPU，還有愈來愈多DRAM、高頻寬記憶體（HBM）和NAND，記憶體晶片正在從便宜的標準商品，變成被優先分配的稀缺資源。據摩根士丹利（Morgan Stanley）最新研究報告指出，這一輪儲存漲價稱為

items.title: 三星健康平台大改版　新款Galaxy Watch將導入AI生命徵象監測
items.summary: 三星電子為加速搶攻數位健康市場，將改版旗下行動健康管理平台「Samsung Health」，而透過此次改版，2026年下半新一代Galaxy Watch將率先搭載AI生命徵象監測、心臟健康分數及聽力保護等新功能。根據韓媒

items.title: 【Amy & Dr. Chip】微軟洽購AI新創Inception　布局「後OpenAI時代」
items.summary: 據傳，微軟（Microsoft）正著手準備「後OpenAI時代」，尋求收購人工智慧（AI）新創以強化技術與人才，目前鎖定Inception。Inception專注於加速擴散模型技術，預計售價逾10億美元，Elon Musk旗下SpaceX也

items.title: 科技1分鐘： 玻璃龍頭披上AI星「光」——康寧光通訊發展概況
items.summary: AI高算力需求爆發，擁有175年歷史的玻璃材料大廠康寧（Corning），正憑藉深厚的光纖技術以及超過17年的光通訊經驗，跨越傳統零組件供應商角色，從材料供應商轉型AI資料中心光通訊解決方案的重要推手。光通訊主

items.title: 晶片通膨重塑手機版圖　蘋果、三星相對受益
items.summary: 隨著半導體價格持續攀升，「記憶體成本」已成2026年下半智慧型手機市場最大變數。近來市場觀察指出，中國大陸中低階手機廠商因成本結構較脆弱，已開始縮減出貨量，蘋果（Apple）與三星電子（Samsung

items.title: 資料中心建設3年週期太慢　南韓KT Cloud模組化建設、數位分身突破
items.summary: 韓國電信大廠KT電信透過旗下雲端子公司KT Cloud，正積極拓展AI資料中心事業。不過相關人士指出，主要科技業者的需求規格正以每隔數月便換代的速度迭代，且必須在兩年內完工交付，南韓資料中心建設周期過長將

items.title: 歐盟AI超大型資料中心計畫卡關　企業夥伴參與意願降溫
items.summary: 歐盟（EU）原定投入200億歐元（約233億美元）、在境內興建5座大型AI資料中心的計畫，正面臨脫軌危機，更讓部分潛在合作夥伴萌生退意。據彭博（Bloomberg）報導，歐盟2025年宣布支持打造「超大型AI工廠」

items.title: 樂金集團首度大規模採購NVIDIA GPU　推進實體AI領域
items.summary: 樂金集團（LG Group）傳將自NVIDIA引進1萬張Blackwell GPU，計劃用於加速集團AI轉型。值得注意的是，樂金集團過去雖曾引進NVIDIA GPU，但這次是首次進行大規模採購。根據韓媒每日經濟報導，業界消息指

items.title: AMD 百億美元布局台灣 AI 生態系，AIC 於 COMPUTEX 展示 Helios 機架平台
items.summary: 隨著 AI 基礎設施需求高速成長，AMD 宣布將加碼投資台灣 AI 生態系，總規模上看百億美元，進一步深化與台 […]

items.title: South Korea data center market to hit KRW11T by 2029
items.summary: South Korea's private data center market is set to expand rapidly as generative AI demand drives a global investment boom, with AI infrastructure, power supply, geographic dispersion, and data sovereignty reshaping the industry's development path. Jong-hyun Shin, a researcher at the Korea Data Center Council (KDCC), said 2028 is expected to be the peak year for data centers finishing construction.

items.title: Zhen Ding posts strongest May revenue as server optical module shipments surge
items.summary: Zhen Ding Technology Group reported that May consolidated revenue reached a 2026 high as robust demand for high-end AI products drove significant year-over-year growth, the company said. The firm attributed the performance to a sharp uptick in server and optical module shipments, which rose nearly 2.5 times year-over-year, and strong momentum in IC substrate sales, which grew nearly 80% year-over-year.

items.title: Pegatron unit ASRock Rack wins order to supply 587 GPU servers for Thailand AI data center
items.summary: ASRock Rack, a subsidiary of Pegatron Group's ASRock Inc., has won an order to supply 587 GPU servers equipped with Nvidia B200 accelerators to Japan-based AI infrastructure provider Datasection Inc., the two firms announced. The systems are contracted for deployment at Datasection's AI data center near Bangkok, Thailand, and will serve as a dedicated high-performance computing platform for US companies.

items.title: Samsung Foundry turns to 5nm, 8nm orders as 2nm comeback takes shape
items.summary: Samsung Electronics is pursuing a two-track foundry strategy, accelerating its 2nm push for future customers while asking partners to step up sales of its 5nm and 8nm processes to support near-term utilization, according to ZDNet Korea.

items.title: JCET opens 3D packaging plant to target AI power modules, CPO demand
items.summary: JCET has opened a new advanced manufacturing facility at its Chengdong production base in Jiangyin, strengthening the Chinese OSAT provider's push into advanced packaging for AI computing, power modules, and next-generation data centers.

items.title: BOE says glass-based packaging substrate pilot line has yet to reach mass-production yield
items.summary: BOE Technology Group's push into glass-based packaging substrates remains short of mass production, even as the Chinese display maker expands into new materials and advanced display technologies to build growth beyond conventional panels.

items.title: Transcend says Samsung DDR4 supply secured through 2027 as memory prices rise
items.summary: Transcend Information said revenue for April and May combined has already surpassed its first-quarter total, as tight memory supply and elevated prices continue to lift sales at the Taiwan-based memory module maker.

items.title: Sercomm posts record first five months as North America drives 2026 revenue surge
items.summary: Sercomm Corporation reported May 2026 revenue of NT$6.48 billion (approx. US$205 million), up 70% year-over-year, and said cumulative revenue from January to May reached NT$30 billion, a 55.3% increase that set new company records for both monthly and year-to-date sales. The telecom broadband equipment maker attributed the gains to stronger North American demand and accelerating orders tied to AI-driven network traffic growth, executives said.

items.title: China’s space-computing push draws chipmakers, satellite firms, and rocket companies
items.summary: As artificial intelligence drives an insatiable demand for computing power, China is beginning to look beyond terrestrial data centers and edge computing toward a new frontier: space.

items.title: ADATA goes global with data centers, eyes 1Q27 payoff
items.summary: Strong demand for memory procurement from AI servers continues, and ADATA chairman Simon Chen believes the AI expansion cycle is expected to sustain strong growth for at least another four to five years. As a result, ADATA is adding AI data center construction as a new business focus, working with strategic partners to deploy projects in four countries, including Brazil and Malaysia. The company expects this move to significantly increase its AI value content, reduce reliance on memory industry

items.title: Electric bus with e-paper exterior displays hits road
items.summary: E-paper applications are moving beyond retail electronic shelf labels and e-readers into the smart mobility market. BMW's color-changing car that features E Ink's electrophoretic displays technology has cleared regulatory hurdles and is nearing mass production. The Taiwan-based E Ink has also teamed up with King Lung Auto Manufacturing and several other local suppliers to unveil the world's first electric bus equipped with an e-paper display system at Computex 2026.

items.title: Aewin pushes two-phase direct liquid cooling for high-density AI racks and gaming
items.summary: Aewin announced a push into two-phase direct liquid cooling to address rising data center thermal demands as rack power consumption moves past 100kW, with demonstrations and product plans shown at COMPUTEX 2026 and a roadmap targeting commercial shipments in 2026 and scaled volume in 2027. The company positioned its two-phase direct liquid cooling, or 2P DLC, as a core element of a broader cooling stack that spans data centers to edge sites and as an infrastructure contributor to Qisda Group&rsq

items.title: Taiwan Mobile and GMI Cloud sign MOU to expand AI data center services into SEA and beyond
items.summary: Taiwan Mobile announced at Computex 2026 that it signed a memorandum of understanding (MOU) with GMI Cloud to expand AI data center and high-end compute services across emerging markets in Southeast Asia, Northeast Asia, the Americas, and Europe. The firms said the agreement aims to deliver AI data center offerings that comply with local regulations and cybersecurity standards while competing for cross-border compute business.

items.title: AI 股漲翻天還能買誰？Barron′s：輝達等五檔仍算便宜，就算退潮也是留到最後那批
items.summary: 如果你今年投資也搭上這波晶片熱潮，現在或許該停下來問一句：手上的股票，是真材實料，還是隨浪起舞？ 漲勢「不分好 […]

items.title: AMD 不急推 AM6，AM5 平台將一路支援至 2029 年
items.summary: 根據 Wccftech 報導，AMD 表示將持續延長 AM5 平台生命周期至 2029 年，未來除了 Zen  […]

items.title: 護國神山還在長高、造就千萬年薪族，台灣消費信心為何停滯不前？
items.summary: 英國《經濟學人》指出，AI 浪潮與台積電帶動台灣出口與 GDP 飆升，但傳統產業衰退、薪資差距擴大、青年低薪、 […]

items.title: Qisda accelerates pivot into AI solutions provider
items.summary: Qisda is accelerating its transformation into an AI solutions provider amid the intensifying AI race. President Cally Ko has highlighted changes to the company's AI roadmap since taking the helm, aiming to build a full AI ecosystem spanning data centers, edge computing, and vertical applications by integrating hardware and software resources across the group.

items.title: Advantech pushes ecosystem strategy as edge AI gains momentum
items.summary: As artificial intelligence moves beyond cloud-based chatbots and into factories, hospitals and warehouses, industrial computing company Advantech is positioning itself at the center of what it sees as the next phase of the AI revolution: the rise of edge and physical AI.

items.title: AI smart glasses brand breaks Japan crowdfunding record, adds Gemini Flash 3.5 and enters Australia
items.summary: China-based AI smart eyewear brand Rokid has accelerated its global expansion and technological roadmap, supported by a wave of momentum across its hardware and open AI ecosystem. From major software upgrades to record-breaking market demand and new regional territory launches, the company is solidifying its position at the forefront of spatial computing.

items.title: AI demand squeezes mainstream DRAM supply as DDR5, DDR4 prices rise
items.summary: DRAM prices are climbing as Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, and Micron Technology prioritize high-bandwidth memory, or HBM, for AI applications, tightening supply of mainstream DDR5 and DDR4 products used in PCs, servers, and other electronics, according to Nikkei.

items.title: Nvidia built its empire on gamers. Jensen Huang just reminded the world why that still matters
items.summary: Jensen Huang arrived in Seoul on June 5 for what looked like a replay of his Taiwan trip — meetings with Samsung Electronics, SK Hynix, LG Group and Hyundai Motor Group, all companies whose strengths in HBM, advanced packaging, autonomous driving, robotics and smart factories are increasingly tied to Nvidia's future. But his first stop was none of them.

items.title: Jensen Huang shouts 'Everyone loves HBM' at Seoul barbecue as Nvidia announces Korea R&D center and previews four new products
items.summary: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang dined with the heads of South Korea's largest technology conglomerates in Hongdae on June 5, turning a barbecue dinner into one of the most theatrical corporate appearances of his Korea trip.

items.title: From Taiwan's supply chain to South Korea's Faker: how Jensen Huang reads different markets
items.summary: Jensen Huang wrapped up his Taiwan run at GTC Taipei and Computex 2026 and flew to South Korea on June 5. His first stop was not Samsung or SK Hynix. It was Hongdae, where he met T1 star Faker — Lee Sang-hyeok — co-signed an RTX 5090 graphics card, and immediately set the internet on fire.

items.title: 輝達、聯發科、高通搶攻筆電晶片！PC 市場飽和論紛飛，巨頭仍參戰
items.summary: PC 市場低迷已久，換機潮沒來，出貨量還在跌；但 COMPUTEX 期間，輝達、聯發科、高通等巨頭接連宣布殺入 […]

items.title: 力積電 5 月營收達 57.7 億元，連續兩個月站上 50 億元大關
items.summary: 晶圓代工廠力積電公布最新營收數據，受惠於記憶體與邏輯代工報價調漲效益，5月合併營收達57.7億元，使得營收金額 […]

items.title: OECD data show China-based semiconductor makers receive outsized industrial support
items.summary: Industrial subsidies reached their highest level since the global financial crisis in 2023 and 2024, according to a June 2026 OECD release based on its new MAGIC database of industrial subsidies. The database tracks support received by 525 of the world's largest manufacturing groups across 15 industrial sectors from 2005 to 2024.

items.title: Metalenses move from lab concept to robotics opportunity
items.summary: As artificial intelligence (AI) drives demand for more powerful computing systems and smarter machines, Taiwanese optics manufacturer Ability Opto-Electronics Technology is positioning itself at the intersection of two emerging technology frontiers: metalenses and optical interconnects.

items.title: Samsung's biggest union loses majority status after bonus talks
items.summary: Samsung Electronics is facing a new shift in labor relations after the Samsung Electronics branch of the Samsung Group Super-Enterprise Union reportedly lost its status as a majority union following a sharp drop in membership after bonus negotiations. The move weakens the union's representation and could further fragment future labor talks and union power at Samsung Electronics.

items.title: Foxconn posts record May revenue as AI rack demand fuels growth
items.summary: Computex 2026 closed on June 5 with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang reaffirming the scale of AI infrastructure demand in his keynote — and Foxconn, as the world's largest electronics contract manufacturer, is among the most direct beneficiaries of that buildout.

items.title: NXP chief says factories will lead robot adoption globally
items.summary: Industrial factories are likely to become the first major market for robots at scale, according to Rafael Sotomayor, NXP President and CEO, who said manufacturers want stable systems, dependable performance, and clear financial returns. This view suggests that the earliest gains from robotics may come in places where efficiency improvements are easiest to measure.

items.title: US officials explore an AI dividend for American households
items.summary: Senior US officials have begun exploring a highly unconventional idea: taking equity stakes in leading artificial intelligence companies and using the proceeds to share the gains of the AI boom with the American public.

items.title: Alchip Accelerates on AI ASIC Demand
items.summary:
Alchip Technologies reported improved financial results for the first quarter, reflecting continued momentum in advanced artificial intelligence (AI), high-performance computing (HPC), and custom ASIC demand. The company cited stronger customer engagement in leading-edge semiconductor designs and accelerating tape-out… Read More

The post

items.title: Intel: Pushing EMIB Forward: Design Methodology Insights with Synopsys Tools
items.summary: As advanced packaging becomes a critical enabler for next-generation semiconductor products, Intel continues to drive innovation through its Embedded Multi-die Interconnect Bridge (EMIB) technology. EMIB has emerged as a foundational packaging solution for heterogeneous integration, allowing multiple chiplets and… Read Mor
